content:
- Finished the full task 04 implementation and verification.
- Added the published `source-bootstrap` image boundary, rewrote the README novice path around pulled GHCR images only, split README public-contract assertions out of runner docker harness helpers, and expanded the master-image workflow plus CI contract to publish both novice-path images.
- Converted the README-owned source-bootstrap fixture check to the image surface, kept the bootstrap contract focused on the canonical YAML fixture, and confirmed the public contract no longer relies on `cargo run` or local `docker build`.
- Required lanes are green: `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`; `make test-long` was not required because this task did not change ignored long tests or their selection boundary.
- Updated the task file and plan, ran `.ralph/task_switch.sh`, and next I am committing every changed file and pushing the task-finished commit.

- Read the prior codex progress entry, refreshed Ralph summaries with `.ralph/update_task_summary.sh`, and read `.ralph/current_tasks.md`, `.ralph/current_tasks_done.md`, `.ralph/ralph-choose-task.md`, and the `list-active-tasks` skill instructions.
- Resolved backlog gating from the actual task files: there are no active failing bug tasks, no active priority tags, no active meta-task, and only one blocked item (`story-22a/02`, blocked by `story-22a/01`). All other active items are unblocked failing non-bug tasks.
- Determined the current architecture from the codebase before picking: the repo is now a Rust workspace (`crates/runner`, `crates/source-bootstrap`) plus a pruned Go-based `cockroachdb_molt` verify slice. Story 18 tasks 01-04 are already done, which makes `story-18/.../05-task-build-an-ultra-scoped-http-job-api-for-single-active-verify-runs.md` the highest-leverage unblocked next step because it turns the existing verify image/config slice into the actual remotely triggerable verify service that later story-18 work builds on. Story 16 task 05 remains important but is documentation cleanup rather than the current architectural frontier.
- Next after quitting: keep `.ralph/current_task.txt` pointed at story 18 task 05 and start that task with the `tdd` skill.

- Read prior progress, confirmed `.ralph/current_task.txt` points at story-18 verify-http-image task 05, and re-read the required `improve-code-boundaries` and `tdd` skill instructions.
- Next I am reading the task file and any existing plans to determine whether this turn is plan-only (`TO BE VERIFIED`) or execution (`NOW EXECUTE`).

- Read task 05 and confirmed it is still unplanned (`<status>not_started</status>`, no existing `_plans` dir entry).
- This turn is therefore a planning turn. Next I am reading the preceding task's plan plus the current verify service code layout to design the HTTP/job interfaces and identify at least one boundary cleanup for `improve-code-boundaries`.

- Read the prior task-04 plan and confirmed task-file convention: completed tasks store the plan path in a `<plan>...</plan>` tag.
- Next I am reading the current `verifyservice` config/command code plus the existing `verify` command and engine so the new task-05 plan can name the concrete runtime and test seams instead of hand-waving.

- Read the current `verifyservice` package and confirmed task 05 starts from config-validation only: there is no HTTP runtime, no in-memory job store, and no persisted verify result shape yet.
- Next I am tracing existing inconsistency/reporting types and any `verify-service` mentions so the plan can reuse the existing verify output boundary instead of inventing a second result schema.

- Traced existing verify output ownership to `verify/inconsistency`: there are already typed status/mismatch/summary objects, so the HTTP layer should capture those directly and render JSON once instead of parsing logs.
- Next I am checking for existing HTTP/JSON testing patterns and the current name-filter boundary so the plan can keep request inputs tightly scoped and non-duplicated.

- Confirmed there is no existing dedicated HTTP runtime pattern in the Go verify slice, so task 05 needs a clean runtime package under `verifyservice` instead of stuffing handlers into Cobra.
- I hit one small lookup miss (`utils/filter.go`); the actual filter contract lives in `utils/obj_filter.go`. After reading that I have enough context to write the plan and, if the interface still looks coherent, flip it to `NOW EXECUTE`.

- The plan is nearly ready. I am checking the existing Rust verify-image contract tests because task 05 probably needs to extend the published-image surface for `verify-service run` without reopening the old runner boundary.

- Created the task-05 plan at `.ralph/tasks/story-18-verify-http-image/05-task-build-an-ultra-scoped-http-job-api-for-single-active-verify-runs_plans/2026-04-19-verify-http-job-api-plan.md` and recorded that path in the task file.
- The plan is execution-ready and ends in `NOW EXECUTE`. It defines the HTTP contract (`POST /jobs`, `GET /jobs/{job_id}`, `POST /stop`), single-active in-memory job registry, typed result capture from `inconsistency.Reporter`, request-scoped filter handling, TLS listener ownership, and the main boundary cleanup: remove service dependence on `cmdutil` global filter state.
- Next after quitting immediately: read the `tdd` skill again as required for `NOW EXECUTE`, then execute the plan slice-by-slice with red/green tests first, run `go test ./...`, `make check`, `make lint`, and `make test`, and only then update task completion metadata.

- Read the current task file and the execution plan for story-18 task 05.
- Confirmed the task is in NOW EXECUTE state and the plan expects slice-by-slice TDD for POST /jobs, GET /jobs/{job_id}, POST /stop, a single-active in-memory registry, typed reporter capture, and request-scoped filters.
- Re-read the required improve-code-boundaries and tdd skills from disk. Next I am inspecting the current verifyservice, verify command, and inconsistency reporter code to start the first red/green slice against the planned public HTTP surface.

- Added the first verifyservice HTTP tracer-bullet test and made it pass.
- Introduced a minimal verifyservice runtime boundary with NewService, injected Runner dependencies, a POST /jobs handler, in-memory single-active tracking, deterministic ID injection, and cleanup via Service.Close.
- Next I am adding the next red/green slice for GET /jobs/{job_id} so live job lookup becomes externally observable before I broaden into stop semantics and result capture.

- Completed several red/green HTTP slices in verifyservice: POST /jobs, GET /jobs/{job_id}, 409 on concurrent starts, /stop without job_id, completed result capture, failed result capture, and scoped filter request decoding.
- Added a new verifyservice-owned request/filter boundary plus typed in-memory result capture instead of parsing logs. Extended utils filtering to support include+exclude semantics for request-scoped filters.
- Next I need to wire the real config-backed verify runner and verify-service run command, then add targeted stop/invalid-regex coverage, command/help/image contract updates, and finally run make check/lint/test.

- Implemented story-18 task 05 end-to-end: verify-service run command, in-memory single-active HTTP job API, typed result capture, request-scoped include/exclude filters, stop semantics, restart-loss behavior, TLS bootstrap, and verify image entrypoint/contract updates.
- Verified the task gates are green: make check, make lint, make test. Also ran focused Go tests for verifyservice/cmd/verifyservice/utils and cargo test -p runner --test verify_image_contract. A broader go test ./... in cockroachdb_molt/molt still fails in legacy packages that expect local DB services; that is documented in the plan and is not a task completion gate.
- Next I am updating Ralph state via task_switch, then I need to commit every staged change and push the branch with a task-finished message before quitting.
